title: Tapping for the Morning Scale
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the number that sets the mood before the day starts
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 12 of Rebecca's WEIGHT LOSS program. The script addresses the weight on the scale as a mood-setter: the number that can make or break the morning before anything else has happened.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 12 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use on mornings when the scale number has dictated how the day is going before it starts. Good when weighing has become a daily anxiety ritual rather than useful information.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 12 of Rebecca's WEIGHT LOSS program. The script addresses the way the morning scale number sets the emotional tone for the whole day: the number determines how good or bad the day is going to feel before it has started.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though the number on the scale is running the morning, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the wrong number can ruin my whole day before it has started, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I keep stepping on it even when it does not help, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I checked the scale this morning.
**Side of eye:** The number is running the morning.
**Under eye:** Good number and I feel good. Bad number and the day is already gone.
**Under nose:** One number deciding everything is too much power.
**Chin:** I am more than a daily fluctuation.
**Collarbone:** The number is information. It is not a verdict.
**Under arm:** I want to take the scale's power back.
**Top of head:** A number cannot tell me who I am.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** The number is one data point among many.
**Side of eye:** My body changes throughout the day and throughout the week.
**Under eye:** I can choose how much weight this number gets.
**Under nose:** My worth is not on the scale.
**Chin:** I can have a good day regardless of what the scale says.
**Collarbone:** Information, not identity.
**Under arm:** I can put the scale down and get on with the day.
**Top of head:** This morning is mine. The number does not own it.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Adapted from Day 12 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
